label: GFC_peak_fall
weakest: dim1: bull-lag numbers (6.8% vs 8.3%, 8.6% vs 16.8%) are repeated verbatim across all 10 notes; the surrounding frame ('exactly the scenario the structural drag was meant to fund') is week-specific enough to earn 6 but is the thinnest margin in this note

label: GFC_trough
weakest: dim1: same fixed numbers reused; context ('market's worst point in a generation...irrelevant') is genuinely differentiated but the numeric pair is structurally identical to every other note — closest dimension to a deduction

label: dotcom_bear
weakest: dim1: 6.8%/8.3% and 8.6%/16.8% pair appears verbatim again; 'buying the strategy's thesis wholesale' is week-specific framing that keeps it at 6, but this is the note's tightest dimension

label: covid_crash
weakest: dim1: fixed number pair repeated; 'crash compressing months of drawdown into weeks' is velocity-specific and genuinely contextualizes the week, keeping it at 6 — still the weakest dimension by margin

label: bull_2017
weakest: dim1: number pair repeated; 'near-all-time-high, no-volatility week' framing is week-specific, but this is the weakest dimension — the SGOV-vs-rally explanation is near-identical in wording to bull_2021

label: bull_2021
weakest: dim1: 'fresh 52-week high and 200d MA well below price, textbook week' is specific; however the sentence structure and examples mirror bull_2017 closely — the weakest dimension, though still above the boilerplate threshold

label: bull_2013_qend
weakest: dim1: '−0.71% drawdown in a post-2009 bull run' is week-specific context, but the parenthetical referencing 2008 and 2020 crashes is a recurring aside in bull notes — weakest dimension by margin

label: mild_dip_2018
weakest: dim1: number pair verbatim again; 'With the S&P off −17.54% and now below its 200d MA...funded $110K in dry powder' is well-contextualized; weakest dimension is dim1 by the same thin margin as other notes

label: qend_2019
weakest: dim1: 'Ending 2019 at essentially a 52-week high' is week-specific; second half references 'the year-end quarter-end rebalance check as maintenance' which is slightly formulaic but genuinely tied to the quarter-end context — weakest by margin

label: near_high_2020
weakest: dim1: 'mid-January 2020 — just weeks before the COVID crash' is prescient and week-specific framing not repeated elsewhere; overall the strongest bull-note differentiation, but the number pair is still verbatim — weakest dimension by margin
